ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general doughnut food product class; no specific sugar-raised yeast donut subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EKX covers doughnuts as a category; no sugar-raised variant subcode exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for sugar-raised donut found in published SNOMED CT release.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard sugar-raised yeast donut weighing approximately 60 g, as typically served at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, with sugar glaze (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 18249)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for yeast-leavened sugar-glazed doughnut used as the closest defensible match. The Dunkin' Sugar Raised donut is a yeast-raised dough deep-fried and dusted with granulated sugar, which is nutritionally analogous to the USDA yeast-leavened sugar-glazed doughnut category. Values are per 100 g as reported by USDA.

gi_source.title: Doughnut, cake-type and yeast-raised — GI values from the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008). International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283. Yeast-raised doughnut GI reported as approximately 76 (high). Sugar Raised donut is a yeast-leavened deep-fried dough dusted with sugar, which is nutritionally analogous. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 47 × 0.60) / 100 = 21.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (340 mg per 100 g) and refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of sugar-raised donuts due to moderate sodium content (approximately 204 mg per standard 60 g serving) and the high refined carbohydrate content, which may contribute to weight gain and elevated blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (76) and high refined sugar and carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit sugar-raised donuts as the high GI of 76 and approximately 28 g of carbohydrates per 60 g serving will cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, portion size must be tightly controlled and paired with low-GI foods.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and high refined carbohydrate content from deep-frying in shortening
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of sugar-raised donuts due to the presence of saturated fat (4.5 g per 100 g), trans fat (0.2 g per 100 g), and high refined carbohydrate content, all of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles and increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A Sugar Raised Donut (also called Sugar Raised Doughnut) is a classic yeast-leavened deep-fried doughnut that is dusted or coated with granulated white sugar after frying. The dough is made from enriched wheat flour, sugar, yeast, eggs, milk, and vegetable shortening, which is proofed and then deep-fried in oil until golden brown. The exterior is light and slightly crisp from the frying process, while the interior is soft, airy, and pillowy due to the yeast leavening. The sugar coating provides a sweet, slightly gritty texture on the surface. This item is sold at Dunkin' Donuts outlets in Singapore, including the Funan location, and is a widely recognised Western-style bakery snack. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 374 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content (47 g per 100 g) and moderate fat content (18 g per 100 g) from deep-frying. The glycemic index is high (approximately 76), making it a food that causes rapid blood glucose elevation. It is not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. It contains gluten, eggs, milk, and soy allergens and is not suitable for vegans. It is classified as an ultra-processed food.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Dunkin Donuts Sugar Raised donut?
Is a sugar raised donut healthy?
What is the GI of a yeast donut?
Sugar raised donut nutrition facts Singapore
Can diabetics eat sugar raised donuts?
What is a sugar raised doughnut made of?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Dunkin' Donuts Singapore Sugar Raised donut exists in an approved primary source. Values are adapted from the USDA SR Legacy entry for yeast-leavened sugar-glazed doughnut (NDB 18249), which is the closest defensible analogue in an approved secondary global source. GI is estimated from the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables for yeast-raised doughnuts. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact formulation of the Dunkin' Singapore product is not independently verified against an approved source.
notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (yeast-leavened, sugar-glazed doughnut) as no Singapore-specific or Dunkin'-specific approved-source entry exists. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. Vitamin C is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — yeast-raised doughnuts contain no meaningful vitamin C. GI of 76 is sourced from Atkinson et al. (2008) international GI tables for yeast-raised doughnuts (analogous estimate). GL per serving = (76 × 47 × 0.60) / 100 = 21.4 g for a 60 g serving. The food is sold at Dunkin' Donuts Funan, Singapore, as listed on field survey data. Cuisine tags from input (Coffee, Donut, Bagel) reflect the restaurant category on the delivery platform rather than the food's own cuisine classification. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context as the food is sold in Singapore, following G6 rules for Singapore-served foods. The food_name has been expanded from 'Sugar Raised' to 'Sugar Raised Donut' to provide a canonical English identity.
